questions.question: Define the term 'congruence' and explain its meaning in geometry.
questions.answer: Congruence is the relationship between two figures that are exactly the same in shape and size. In geometry, two figures are congruent if they are copies of each other and can be superimposed to cover each other perfectly.

questions.question: Give three examples of congruent objects we see in our daily lives.
questions.answer: 1. Two ₹5 coins of the same year. 2. Two identical biscuits from the same packet. 3. Two pages of the same notebook. These objects are congruent because they have the same dimensions and shape.

questions.question: Explain the congruence of line segments with an example.
questions.answer: Two line segments are congruent if they have the same length. If line segment AB = 7 cm and CD = 7 cm, then AB ≅ CD. If they have different lengths, they are not congruent.

questions.question: What is the condition for two angles to be congruent?
questions.answer: Two angles are congruent if they have the same measure. For example, if ∠A = 60° and ∠B = 60°, then ∠A ≅ ∠B. The length of the arms of the angle does not affect congruence.

questions.question: Explain what it means for two triangles to be congruent.
questions.answer: Two triangles are congruent if they are copies of each other. When superimposed, their corresponding vertices, sides, and angles match exactly. This is denoted by the symbol ≅.

questions.question: Define 'Correspondence' in the context of congruent triangles.
questions.answer: Correspondence refers to the specific pairing of vertices, sides, and angles between two triangles. For ΔABC ≅ ΔPQR, the vertex A corresponds to P, B to Q, and C to R.

questions.question: State and explain the SSS congruence criterion.
questions.answer: SSS stands for Side-Side-Side. If three sides of one triangle are equal to the three corresponding sides of another triangle, then the triangles are congruent.

questions.question: Draw and explain a case where SSS congruence is applied.
questions.answer: In ΔABC and ΔXYZ, if AB=XY, BC=YZ, and AC=XZ, then ΔABC ≅ ΔXYZ by SSS criterion. This ensures the two triangles are identical 'twins'.

questions.question: Explain the SAS congruence criterion.
questions.answer: SAS stands for Side-Angle-Side. If two sides and the angle included between them of one triangle are equal to the corresponding sides and included angle of another triangle, they are congruent.

questions.question: Why must the angle be 'included' in the SAS criterion?
questions.answer: The angle must be 'included' (the angle between the two sides) to fix the third side's length. If the equal angle is not between the two equal sides, the triangles might not be congruent.

questions.question: Define the ASA congruence criterion.
questions.answer: ASA stands for Angle-Side-Angle. If two angles and the included side of one triangle are equal to the two corresponding angles and included side of another triangle, they are congruent.

questions.question: What is the 'included side' in ASA congruence?
questions.answer: The included side is the side that forms the common arm for both the specified angles. In ΔABC, if we take ∠B and ∠C, the included side is BC.

questions.question: State the RHS congruence criterion.
questions.answer: RHS stands for Right angle-Hypotenuse-Side. If the hypotenuse and one side of a right-angled triangle are equal to the hypotenuse and one side of another right-angled triangle, they are congruent.

questions.question: What are the three mandatory parts to check for RHS congruence?
questions.answer: 1. Both must have a Right angle (90°). 2. Their Hypotenuses must be equal. 3. Any one of the other two Sides must be equal.

questions.question: If ΔABC ≅ ΔPQR, list all the corresponding equal parts.
questions.answer: Sides: AB=PQ, BC=QR, AC=PR. Angles: ∠A=∠P, ∠B=∠Q, ∠C=∠R. Vertices: A↔P, B↔Q, C↔R.

questions.question: Can two triangles with the same area always be congruent? Explain.
questions.answer: No, two triangles with the same area are not always congruent. Congruence requires the same shape and size, whereas area only measures space. A triangle with base 4 and height 3 has area 6, and a triangle with base 6 and height 2 also has area 6, but they are not congruent.

questions.question: Is AAA a criterion for congruence? Why or why not?
questions.answer: No, AAA (Angle-Angle-Angle) is not a congruence criterion. Two triangles can have the same angles but different sizes (one can be an enlarged version of the other).

questions.question: Explain the difference between SSS and SAS criteria.
questions.answer: SSS requires all three sides of both triangles to be equal. SAS requires only two sides to be equal, provided the angle between them is also equal.

questions.question: If two squares have the same area, are they congruent? Justify.
questions.answer: Yes. If squares have the same area, their sides must be equal ($Side = \sqrt{Area}$). Since all angles in a square are 90°, if the sides are equal, the squares are congruent.

questions.question: What is the importance of the Hypotenuse in RHS congruence?
questions.answer: In a right-angled triangle, the hypotenuse is the longest side. In RHS congruence, the equality of the hypotenuse is a specific requirement that distinguishes it from the SAS criterion.

questions.question: Describe the relation between congruent triangles and their perimeters.
questions.answer: If two triangles are congruent, their perimeters must be equal because all their corresponding sides are equal in length, and perimeter is the sum of all sides.

questions.question: How do you denote that ΔPQR is a 'twin' of ΔABC?
questions.answer: We use the congruence symbol ≅. It is written as ΔABC ≅ ΔPQR. This symbol combines '=' (equal size) and '~' (same shape).

questions.question: Explain the concept of 'Superposition' with respect to two congruent circles.
questions.answer: Two circles are congruent if they have the same radius. Superposition means that if you place one circle over the other, they will coincide perfectly, with no part of either circle sticking out.

questions.question: Why is 'Geometric Twins' a good name for congruent triangles?
questions.answer: Twins look exactly alike and have the same characteristics. Similarly, congruent triangles have the same shape and size, making them identical in every geometric aspect.

questions.question: List the four main criteria for triangle congruence learned in Class 7.
questions.answer: The four criteria are: 1. SSS (Side-Side-Side), 2. SAS (Side-Angle-Side), 3. ASA (Angle-Side-Angle), and 4. RHS (Right angle-Hypotenuse-Side).
